back_translation: Joseph went and told Pharaoh, 'My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that is theirs, have come from the land of Canaan; behold, they are now in the land of Goshen.'
And from among his brothers he chose five men and presented them before Pharaoh.
Pharaoh said to Joseph's brothers, 'What is your occupation?' They said to Pharaoh, 'Your servants are shepherds of flocks — both we and our fathers.'

They said to Pharaoh, 'We have come to sojourn in the land for a time, for there is no pasture for the flocks of your servants, for the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. So please let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen.'
Pharaoh said to Joseph, 'Your father and your brothers have come to you.

The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land. Let them dwell in the land of Goshen. And if you know that there are men of ability among them, set them as chiefs over my livestock.'
Joseph brought in his father Jacob and presented him before Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
Pharaoh said to Jacob, 'How many are the days of the years of your life?'

Jacob said to Pharaoh, 'The days of the years of my sojournings are one hundred and thirty years. Few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not reached the days of the years of the lives of my fathers in their sojournings.'
Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh and went out from before Pharaoh.
Joseph settled his father and his brothers and gave them a holding in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
Joseph provided his father and his brothers and all his father's household with bread according to the count of their little ones.
There was no bread in all the land, for the famine was very severe; the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished because of the famine.
Joseph gathered all the silver found in the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan in exchange for the grain they were buying, and Joseph brought the silver to Pharaoh's house.
When the silver from the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan was gone, all the Egyptians came to Joseph saying, 'Give us bread! Why should we die before your eyes? For the silver is gone.'
Joseph said, 'Bring your livestock, and I will give you bread in exchange for your livestock, since the silver is gone.'

So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them bread in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the herds, and the donkeys. He provided them with bread in exchange for all their livestock that year.

When that year ended, they came to him in the second year and said, 'We will not hide from our lord that the silver is gone, and the herds of livestock belong to our lord. Nothing remains before our lord except our bodies and our land.

Why should we die before your eyes — both we and our land? Buy us and our land in exchange for bread, and we and our land will be slaves to Pharaoh. Give us seed, that we may live and not die, and the land will not become desolate.'
So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, for every Egyptian sold his field, since the famine was strong upon them. The land became Pharaoh's.
And the people he moved to the cities, from one end of the border of Egypt to the other.
Only the land of the priests he did not buy, for the priests had a portion from Pharaoh, and they ate their portion which Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their land.
Joseph said to the people, 'Behold, I have bought you and your land today for Pharaoh. Here is seed for you; sow the land.
At harvest time, you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and four parts shall be yours as seed for the field, food for yourselves, those in your households, and your little ones.'
They said, 'You have saved our lives! May we find favor in the eyes of our lord, and we will be slaves to Pharaoh.'
Joseph established it as a statute to this day on the land of Egypt, that a fifth belongs to Pharaoh. Only the land of the priests by themselves did not become Pharaoh's.
Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. They acquired holdings there and were fruitful and multiplied greatly.
Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; and the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were one hundred forty-seven years.

When the days of Israel drew near to die, he called his son Joseph and said to him, 'If I have found favor in your eyes, please put your hand under my thigh, and show me kindness and truth — please do not bury me in Egypt.
When I lie down with my fathers, you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.' He answered, 'I will do as you have said.'
He said, 'Swear to me.' So he swore to him, and Israel bowed himself at the head of the bed.
